title: gh#57: R1.2 — Implement TwitterPublisher; fold classifyTwitterError inside
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/57.
Backlog section: Candidate 1 — Publisher seam.

## Context

Second PR in Candidate 1. Implements the first concrete Publisher and moves Twitter error classification inside it as a private function. The existing `twitter-publish.service.ts` stays intact — the worker doesn't switch over until R1.4.

## Scope

- Add `packages/worker/src/publishers/twitter.ts` exporting `createTwitterPublisher(): Publisher`
- Implementation reads from `PublishablePost`, decrypts OAuth 1.0a credentials inline (matches existing `twitter-publish.service.ts` discipline — no caching, no logging, function-scope plaintext)
- Move `classifyTwitterError` logic into this file as a private function (delete the export from `@sms/shared/lib/error-classifier` in R1.5)
- Map any caught error to a thrown `PublishFailure` with the correct `kind` + `errorCode` — including token-shape redaction before the message escapes the Publisher
- Phase 4 thread-unsupported behavior: throw `PublishFailure({ kind: 'permanent', errorCode: 'thread_unsupported' })` for now (the lifecycle's pre-flight thread check makes this unreachable in practice, but the Publisher must be safe in isolation)
- Unit tests: success path returns `{ platformPostId }`; permanent and transient failure paths throw `PublishFailure` with correct `kind`; redaction strips token-shaped substrings from messages

## Out of scope

- LinkedIn / Facebook Publishers (R1.3)
- Wiring changes to `publish-worker.ts` (R1.4)
- Removing the old `twitter-publish.service.ts` (R1.5)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` passes
- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker test` passes
- Test fake fixture: `createFakeTwitterPublisher({ result?, error? })` available for downstream tests

## References

- ADRs: [0001](../blob/main/docs/adr/0001-publish-failure-is-thrown-not-returned.md), [0002](../blob/main/docs/adr/0002-publisher-receives-raw-profile-row.md)
- Depends on: #56

history.reasoning: The finding is still open. The original worker evidence path `packages/worker/src/publishers/twitter.ts` does not exist, and there is no `publishers` directory or `createTwitterPublisher`/`createFakeTwitterPublisher` implementation anywhere under `packages/worker` or `packages/shared`. The shared evidence still exists as `packages/shared/src/lib/error-classifier.ts`, and `classifyTwitterError` is still exported there. Current worker code still imports/uses `classifyTwitterError` from `@sms/shared/lib/error-classifier` in `publish-worker.ts` and `post-lifecycle.service.ts`, so the requested move/fold into a TwitterPublisher has not happened. `pnpm --filter @sms/worker typecheck` passed. `pnpm --filter @sms/worker test -- --run` could not start because the read-only sandbox prevented Vitest/Vite from writing its `.vite-temp` config file, so tests do not provide passing evidence in this environment.
commands: rg -n "classifyTwitterError|duplicate_content|auth_revoked|Twitter credentials|Network error contacting Twitter|http_429|error-classifier" packages/shared/src packages/worker/src/__tests__

history.reasoning: The original worker evidence path now exists as packages/worker/src/publishers/twitter.ts. It exports createTwitterPublisher(), defines classifyTwitterError as a private local function, decrypts OAuth 1.0a credential fields inside the publish call path, returns { platformPostId } on success, converts caught errors into PublishFailure with kind/errorCode/httpStatus, redacts token-shaped substrings before exposing messages, and throws permanent thread_unsupported for direct thread posts. The downstream fake fixture also exists as createFakeTwitterPublisher() in the same file. Focused tests now exist at packages/worker/src/__tests__/publishers/twitter.test.ts covering success, permanent failure, transient failure, redaction, thread_unsupported, and the fake fixture. The shared classifier still exists and is still imported by the old worker paths, but the finding explicitly kept twitter-publish.service.ts/worker switchover out of scope and noted shared export removal for R1.5, so that remaining evidence is not enough to keep R1.2 open. pnpm --filter @sms/worker typecheck passed. pnpm --filter @sms/worker build and the focused Vitest command were blocked by the read-only sandbox with EPERM writes to dist/.vite-temp, not by TypeScript or test assertion failures.
